cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Mean Value Theorem: the value $c$ guaranteed to exist on $(a, b)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: MVT conditions: $f$ must satisfy these two conditions on $[a, b]$
cards.answer: f \text{ continuous on } [a,b] \text{ and differentiable on } (a,b)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Extreme Value Theorem: conclusion guaranteed when $f$ is continuous on $[a, b]$
cards.answer: f \text{ attains an absolute maximum and absolute minimum on } [a,b]

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Condition for a critical point at $x = c$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Test for a function increasing on an interval

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Test for a function decreasing on an interval

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Test for concave up on an interval

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Test for concave down on an interval

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Second Derivative Test for a local minimum at $x = c$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Second Derivative Test for a local maximum at $x = c$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Candidates Test: to find absolute extrema on $[a, b]$, evaluate $f$ at these points
cards.answer: f(a),\ f(b),\ \text{and } f(c) \text{ for each critical point } c \in (a,b)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Rolle's Theorem: if $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$, differentiable on $(a,b)$, and $f(a) = f(b)$, then...

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Condition for an inflection point at $x = c$
cards.answer: f''(x) \text{ changes sign at } x = c

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: First Derivative Test: $f$ has a local max at $c$ when $f'$ changes from...

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: First Derivative Test: $f$ has a local min at $c$ when $f'$ changes from...

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Average rate of change formula (also equals MVT slope)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Optimization setup: after setting $f'(c) = 0$, confirm it is a maximum using the Second Derivative Test condition

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: Notation for the $n$-th derivative

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The theorem that guarantees a point in $(a, b)$ where the instantaneous rate of change equals the average rate of change over $[a, b]$ — requires $f$ continuous on $[a,b]$ and differentiable on $(a,b)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The theorem guaranteeing that a continuous function on a closed interval $[a, b]$ must attain both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: A point $x = c$ in the domain of $f$ where $f'(c) = 0$ or $f'(c)$ is undefined — a candidate for local extrema

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The highest value of $f$ in some open interval around $x = c$; also called a local maximum

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The highest value of $f$ over the entire domain or closed interval

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: A point where the function changes from concave up to concave down or vice versa

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The description of a curve that curves upward like a bowl; $f'' > 0$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The description of a curve that curves downward like an arch; $f'' < 0$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The test using the sign of $f'$ to classify critical points as local maxima, minima, or neither

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The test using $f'(c) = 0$ and the sign of $f''(c)$ to classify a critical point as a local max or min

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The method of finding absolute extrema on a closed interval by comparing $f$ at all critical points and endpoints

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The process of finding the maximum or minimum value of a quantity subject to constraints; uses critical points and boundary analysis

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The property describing how a function's derivative is behaving: $f'$ increasing means $f$ is this type

cards.subject: ap-calculus-ab
cards.prompt: The boundary points of a closed interval $[a, b]$ that must be included when finding absolute extrema
